source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design one of four arms of an electric RC quadrotor design-build-vertical-flight benchmark, sized to carry a 500 g payload through an obstacle course while remaining under the FAA Part 107 sUAS envelope, as a multi-part bonded/bolted assembly consisting of the CFRP tube, a central-hub clamp fitting tying the arm root to the frame, a motor mount plate at the tip, and the motor mount fastener set. Per the benchmark RFP: total takeoff mass including payload <= 4.0 kg, battery limited to a single 6S LiPo pack 4000-6000 mAh, hover endurance >= 10 min with payload, payload carry hook mounted at airframe centerline, obstacle course including 1 m x 1 m gates at 2 m altitude. Arm geometry: tubular carbon-fiber (wound CFRP, E = 70 GPa, Poisson 0.30, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile 900 MPa, compressive 700 MPa), 20 mm OD x 16 mm ID x 250 mm length cantilevered from central hub, with motor mount plate at tip carrying a 320 g BLDC motor spinning a 12-inch (305 mm) propeller at up to 9500 rpm producing up to 12.5 N hover thrust and 20 N peak thrust for evasive maneuvers. Load cases: (LC1) hover: steady 12.5 N upward at motor mount plus motor mass 320 g at 1 g; (LC2) max-thrust evasive: 20 N at motor mount plus 2 g lateral on hub representing aggressive roll; (LC3) propeller unbalance: residual 5% unbalance at 9500 rpm = 158 Hz = 1.0 N radial oscillation at motor mount during full endurance 10 min = 9.5e4 cycles; (LC4) hard landing: 1 m drop onto one arm modeled as 10 g impulse at motor mount; (LC5) storage/transport: clip-on wall load 50 N lateral at tip. Pass/fail: (R1) max ply strain below 0.4% under LC1-LC4, (R2) arm deflection at motor plate under LC2 below 8 mm, (R3) first arm bending mode above 50 Hz to stay below 1P = 158 Hz third harmonic sideband and avoid coupling, (R4) arm mass below 45 g each (4 arms contribute to 4.0 kg budget), (R5) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC4 compression above 3.0. Verification is performed on the assembled arm + hub-clamp + motor-plate multi-body model with representative bonded/bolted interface stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, modal, buckling eigenvalue, and mass table.
short_prompt:
Design one of four arms of an electric RC quadrotor design-build-vertical-flight benchmark, sized to carry a 500 g payload within the FAA Part 107 sUAS envelope, as a multi-part bonded/bolted assembly (CFRP tube + central hub clamp + motor mount plate + fasteners).

Total MTOW with payload <= 4.0 kg, single 6S LiPo 4000-6000 mAh, hover endurance >= 10 min with payload, obstacle gates 1 m x 1 m at 2 m altitude. Arm: wound CFRP (E 70 GPa, Poisson 0.30, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile 900 MPa, compressive 700 MPa), 20 mm OD x 16 mm ID x 250 mm length cantilevered. Tip carries a 320 g BLDC motor with a 12 inch (305 mm) prop at up to 9500 rpm producing up to 12.5 N hover / 20 N peak thrust.

Load cases.
- LC1 hover: steady 12.5 N at motor mount + 320 g motor at 1 g.
- LC2 max-thrust evasive: 20 N at motor mount + 2 g lateral on hub.
- LC3 propeller unbalance: 5% at 9500 rpm (158 Hz) = 1.0 N radial for 9.5e4 cycles over 10 min.
- LC4 hard landing: 1 m drop on one arm as 10 g impulse at motor mount.
- LC5 storage: 50 N lateral at arm tip.

First arm bending mode > 50 Hz (below 158 Hz 1P); arm mass below 45 g each.

prompt.title: DBVF quadrotor arm (multi-part bonded/bolted assembly)
prompt.brief: One of four arms of a 4.0 kg MTOW quad: CFRP arm tube + hub clamp + motor mount plate + fasteners carrying a 320 g motor and 12-inch prop.

parts.name: arm_tube
parts.role: CFRP filament-wound tube cantilevered from the central hub to the motor-mount tip

parts.name: hub_clamp_fitting
parts.role: clamp fitting bonding / bolting the arm root into the central frame hub

parts.name: motor_mount_plate
parts.role: plate at arm tip supporting the 320 g BLDC motor and 12-inch propeller

parts.name: motor_mount_fastener_set
parts.role: M3 fasteners attaching the motor mount plate to the arm tube tip

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 250 mm arm cantilever from hub to motor mount plate
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +X outward from hub center along arm axis

boundary_conditions.interfaces: hub clamp bonded + bolted to arm tube root; motor mount plate bonded + bolted to arm tube tip; fasteners modelled as representative stiffness elements
boundary_conditions.support: hub clamp fixed at hub interface for LC1-LC5

prompt.objective: minimize per-arm mass while passing ply-strain, modal, deflection, and buckling gates

interfaces.load_path: arm tube root bonded + clamped into central hub clamp fitting
motor mount plate bonded + bolted to arm tube tip
fastener clamp force tying motor mount to arm tube

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Design-build-vertical-flight quad arms are a CFRP tube bonded/bolted to a central hub clamp and a motor mount plate; hover, evasive, and hard-landing load paths cross both bonded interfaces and must be modelled as a multi-body assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Hub-clamp and motor-plate materials and fastener count are speculative; tube material, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
